episodes.title: Is Grep All You Need? Agent Harness Paper
LongCat-2.0: Open-Source 1M Context That Beats GPT-5.5
episodes.linkedin_post:
Episode 03: 5-minute read. Zero fluff : LongCat-2.0: Open-Source 1M Context That Beats GPT-5.5

A Chinese food delivery company secretly ran the most-used coding model on OpenRouter for 2 months. Nobody knew. They just unmasked it as LongCat-2.0 and open-sourced everything under MIT.

Key findings/points:
- 1.6T parameters, 48B active per token, 1M native context window
- 59.5 on SWE-bench Pro (GPT-5.5 scores 58.6)
- Trained on 50,000 domestic Chinese ASICs - zero Nvidia GPUs
- Available now on OpenRouter at $0.75/M input, full weights on HuggingFace

The takeaway? The best open-source coding model is MIT licensed, cheaper than GPT-5.5, and was hiding in plain sight for 2 months.

https://mimran-khan.github.io/series/demystifying-ai/longcat-2

episodes.title: MCP Goes Stateless: The Biggest Protocol Rewrite Since Launch
episodes.linkedin_post:
Episode 04: 5-minute read. Zero fluff : MCP Goes Stateless: The Biggest Protocol Rewrite Since Launch

MCP ships 7 breaking changes on Jul 28. Sessions removed. Handshake removed. OAuth hardened. HTTP+SSE deprecated. If you only prep for the stateless core, the other 6 will blindside you.

Key findings/points:
- initialize handshake and Mcp-Session-Id completely removed (SEP-2575, SEP-2567)
- Caching with ttlMs replaces server-sent change notifications
- New Extensions framework replaces Roots, Sampling, and Logging
- One migration report: -412 lines, +168 lines - statelessness mostly means deleting code

The takeaway? If you run remote HTTP MCP servers, start testing against the RC now. stdio servers can wait.

https://mimran-khan.github.io/series/demystifying-ai/mcp-stateless

episodes.title: SkillOpt: Your Agent's Skills Are Trainable Parameters
episodes.linkedin_post:
Episode 05: 5-minute read. Zero fluff : SkillOpt: Your Agent's Skills Are Trainable Parameters

Microsoft trained agent skills like neural network weights. +23.5 points on GPT-5.5 without changing a single model parameter. The output? One readable markdown file.

Key findings/points:
- 52/52 evaluation cells: best or tied across 6 benchmarks
- GPT-5.4-mini + skill beats GPT-5.4 without one (small models jump tiers)
- Skills transfer across harnesses: trained in Codex, dropped into Claude Code for +59.7 points
- Median optimized skill is ~920 tokens of plain readable markdown

The takeaway? The closest thing to fine-tuning without fine-tuning - pip install skillopt and try it yourself.

https://mimran-khan.github.io/series/demystifying-ai/skillopt

episodes.title: Antidoom: One LoRA to Kill Your Model's Doom Loops
episodes.linkedin_post:
Episode 06: 5-minute read. Zero fluff : Antidoom: One LoRA to Kill Your Model's Doom Loops

Your reasoning model knows the answer. It just gets stuck repeating "Let me reconsider..." until the context window is exhausted. Liquid AI found the exact token where the loop starts and built a fix.

Key findings/points:
- Qwen3.5-4B doom loop rate: 22.9% drops to 1% with one LoRA adapter
- FTPO (Final Token Preference Optimization) trains on exactly one token position per sample
- Eval scores went UP after training - the model was already capable, just stuck
- Full pipeline runs in hours, one epoch, open-sourced on GitHub

The takeaway? If you run small reasoning models in production, this is a mandatory fix - one command, a few hours, near-zero looping.

https://mimran-khan.github.io/series/demystifying-ai/antidoom

episodes.title: VulnHunter: Capital One Built an Agent That Hacks Your Code First
episodes.linkedin_post:
Episode 07: 5-minute read. Zero fluff : VulnHunter: Capital One Built an Agent That Hacks Your Code First

Traditional SAST scanners dump 400 findings. 90% are false positives. Capital One built something that thinks like a pentester instead. Open-sourced it under Apache 2.0.

Key findings/points:
- Built as Claude Code skills (SKILL.md files) - not a standalone tool
- Three-phase pipeline: discover attack surface, write PoC exploit, generate verified fix
- Every finding ships with a working exploit - if it cannot prove exploitability, it does not report it
- Verification loop re-runs the PoC against patched code - no fix ships without proof

The takeaway? SAST scanners search for patterns. VulnHunter reasons like an adversary. One dumps noise. The other proves the bug is real and hands you a tested fix.

https://mimran-khan.github.io/series/demystifying-ai/vulnhunter

episodes.title: Ring-Zero: Your Model Has Context Anxiety and Nobody Programmed It
episodes.linkedin_post:
Episode 08: 5-minute read. Zero fluff : Ring-Zero: Your Model Has Context Anxiety and Nobody Programmed It

Ant Group trained a 1 trillion parameter model with zero human data. Five cognitive behaviors emerged on their own. One of them is the model panicking about running out of tokens.

Key findings/points:
- Pure RLVR (Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards) - binary reward, zero human labels
- 5 emergent behaviors: anthropomorphism, structured formatting, self-verification, parallel reasoning, context anxiety
- Context anxiety: at ~85% context usage, the model aborts verification and commits to its best answer
- Naive scaling failed - 3 engineering fixes were required before emergence appeared

The takeaway? Nobody taught this model to panic about tokens. Nobody taught it to verify its own answers. These strategies emerged because they maximize correctness at scale. #1 on HuggingFace Daily Papers.

https://mimran-khan.github.io/series/demystifying-ai/ring-zero

episodes.title: Inkling: The Former OpenAI CTO Did What Altman Would Not
episodes.linkedin_post:
Episode 09: 5-minute read. Zero fluff : Inkling: The Former OpenAI CTO Did What Altman Would Not

Mira Murati left OpenAI. Raised $2B. Built a 975B-parameter frontier model from scratch. Then released it fully open under Apache 2.0. No revenue caps. No restrictions. The largest US-built open-weights model.

Key findings/points:
- 975B total params, 41B active per token (MoE), 1M context window, 45T training tokens
- Encoder-free early fusion: text, images, audio enter the same transformer from day one
- SWE-bench Pro: 77.6% - beats every open-weights competitor including Nemotron 3 Ultra
- Apache 2.0 with patent grant - no MAU caps, no use restrictions, no copyleft

The takeaway? Strongest model wins benchmarks. Most open model wins adoption. Murati is betting on adoption. The base model is free. The fine-tuning platform (Tinker) is paid.

https://mimran-khan.github.io/series/demystifying-ai/inkling
